title: [InstCombine] Add function attribute `instcombine-no-verify-fixpoint`
summary:
The generalization strategy involved expanding a specific optimization pattern into a reusable transformation by identifying its core logical equivalence and applying it across diverse contexts. Developers started with a single test case (`computeNumSignBits_add1`) that combined `add`, `icmp`, `llvm.assume`, and `shl` to infer sign bits. They abstracted the pattern's essence: using range information from `icmp` + `assume` to deduce known bits in subsequent operations.

This was generalized by creating a utility function that extracts known bits from any instruction using `computeKnownBits`, then applies the transformation when a shifter's operand has enough known zeros/ones to simplify. The patch shows they extended this to handle various shift types (`shl`, `ashr`, `lshr`) and integrated it into InstCombine's main logic, ensuring it triggers during the fixpoint iteration.

Key steps: 1) Isolate the pattern's core logic (range analysis → known bits), 2) Create a helper that works on arbitrary instructions, 3) Add it to the appropriate combiner (e.g., `visitShl`), 4) Validate with diverse test cases covering edge conditions (different bitwidths, loop phis, complex expressions). This approach turns a one-off optimization into a reusable analysis-driven transformation.
